content: Phase 1: Domain Model & Data Structures. 10 files in lib/domain/. Zod v4 branded types for units. enums.ts + units.ts first, then 6 entity files in parallel, then sample-data.ts + index.ts.

Phase 1 complete. 10 files in lib/domain/: enums.ts, units.ts, river-station.ts, flow-reading.ts, paddling-threshold.ts, official-forecast.ts, correlated-river.ts, weather-window.ts, sample-data.ts, index.ts. All type-check, lint clean, runtime validation passes. Zod v4 branded types for units, z.iso.datetime()/z.iso.date() for timestamps. 3 sample rivers: riviere-du-nord (full), ruisseau-sauvage (partial), torrent-vallee (correlated).

Phase 2 complete. 8 files in lib/weather/. Open-Meteo archive+forecast APIs working. Key finding: snow_depth and soil_moisture are hourly-only variables in Open-Meteo — fetched hourly and aggregated to daily (max for snow_depth, mean for soil_moisture). Wind speed converted from km/h to m/s. Solar radiation converted from MJ/m² to W/m². File cache in .data/weather-cache/ with atomic writes. 730 days fetched for sample station in ~30s, cached re-read in 0.5s. Elevation adjustment applies lapse rate correction.

{"id":"traj-1775396608646-9snl7","task":"Phase 3: Feature Engineering — transform raw weather + station data into ML features for river flow prediction","agent":"architect","steps":[{"action":"spec-analysis","result":"Identified 8 feature categories: precipitation (rolling windows), snowmelt (degree-days, melt potential), soil saturation (trends, percentiles), temperature (freeze/thaw, diurnal range), seasonal (cyclic encoding), catchment (static), lag (autoregressive flow), official forecast (bounds, bias). Key constraint: must work for both training (with observed flow) and prediction (cascading).","quality":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T13:43:42.970Z"},{"action":"architecture-design","result":"Designed 11-file module: types.ts (FeatureRow 46 columns + context), utils.ts (lookback, rollingSum, linearSlope, dayOfYear), 8 feature modules (precip, snowmelt, soil, temp, seasonal, catchment, lag, forecast), compute.ts orchestrator, index.ts barrel. Key decisions: plain number|null for features (no brands), -1 sentinel for unknown catchment, FeatureModule interface pattern, weatherBuffer with 14-day lookback minimum.","quality":0.9,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T13:47:03.612Z"},{"action":"parallel-implementation","result":"Foundation files written (types.ts, utils.ts). Spawning 8 parallel agents for feature modules.","quality":0.9,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T13:47:38.020Z"},{"action":"verification","result":"All 11 files pass: tsc --noEmit (0 errors), eslint (0 errors), runtime test (3 rows × 47 columns from sample data). Key values verified: precip_sum=15mm, snow_depth_change=-3cm (melting), melt_potential=72, soil_trend positive (wetting), freeze_thaw=0, is_spring_melt=1, catchment features populated, flow_prev_1d=24.1, forecast features populated.","quality":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T13:49:35.968Z"}],"startedAt":"2026-04-05T13:43:28.646Z","success":true,"endedAt":"2026-04-05T13:49:38.158Z","feedback":"8 parallel agents for feature modules was effective — all produced correct code on first try. Hierarchical swarm with specialized agents worked well for independent module pattern."}

Phase 3 complete. 11 files in lib/features/. 47-column FeatureRow: precip(7), snowmelt(8), soil(7), temp(4), seasonal(4), catchment(5), lag(4), forecast(6) + 2 metadata. Key patterns: weatherBuffer with 14-day lookback, flowByDate Map for training/prediction mode agnostic lag features, -1 sentinel for unknown catchment, linearSlope for soil trends, n() helper to cast branded types. computeFeatureMatrix() is the main entry point taking station+weather+flow+forecasts → FeatureRow[].

{"id":"traj-1775397220031-5cloyw","task":"Phase 4: Model Training Framework — build training pipeline with gradient boosted trees for river flow prediction","agent":"architect","steps":[{"action":"spec-analysis","result":"Phase 4 spec: gradient boosted trees (XGBoost/LightGBM), temporal train/val/test split (70/15/15), predict log(flow), metrics: NSE, MAPE, threshold accuracy. Multi-horizon training with prediction_horizon as feature. Missing data: -1 sentinel + random feature masking during training. Key decision: need a JS-native gradient boosting implementation since project is TypeScript-only.","quality":0.9,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T13:54:17.558Z"},{"action":"architecture-design","result":"Designed 10-file lib/model/ module. Pure TypeScript CART decision tree + gradient boosting from scratch. Key: NaN-aware splits (XGBoost-style default direction), log(flow) target, MSE loss, temporal split 70/15/15, early stopping, L2 regularization, serialization to JSON. 46 input features (45 from FeatureRow + prediction_horizon). TrainingSample uses Float64Array for efficiency.","quality":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T14:00:40.131Z"},{"action":"verification","result":"All 10 files pass: tsc (0 errors), eslint (0 errors). Runtime verified: metrics (NSE=0.96, MAPE=9.9%, thresholdAcc=80% on hand-crafted data), gradient boosting trains on 140 synthetic samples → NSE=0.96 on test, val loss 1.70→0.056, early stopping at round 33/50. Tree splits work correctly with NaN handling.","quality":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T14:05:35.068Z"}],"startedAt":"2026-04-05T13:53:40.031Z","success":true,"endedAt":"2026-04-05T14:05:37.178Z","feedback":"CART tree + gradient boosting from scratch in TypeScript worked well. 5 parallel agents for independent files (dataset, split, metrics, tree, gradient-boost) was effective. One type mismatch (Float64Array vs number[]) caught and fixed quickly."}

Phase 4 complete. 10 files in lib/model/. Pure TypeScript CART decision tree + gradient boosting from scratch. NaN-aware splits (XGBoost-style default direction). 46 input features (45 from FeatureRow + prediction_horizon). Temporal split 70/15/15. Log(flow) target. MSE loss with L2 regularization. Early stopping. Model serialized to JSON. Verified: synthetic 140-sample training → NSE=0.96 on test, val loss converges 1.70→0.056. Key exports: trainModel(), predictFlow(), hydrateModel(), serializeModel(), deserializeModel().

{"id":"traj-1775397976324-3tecnj","task":"Phase 5: Prediction Engine — produce actionable flow forecasts with confidence bands and threshold crossings","agent":"architect","steps":[{"action":"spec-analysis","result":"Phase 5: cascading prediction (Day+1 uses observed flow, Day+2+ uses predicted), confidence bands (horizon-based widening), correlated river estimation via correlation functions, threshold comparison against paddling levels. Output: DailyForecast[] with flow, confidence interval, threshold status per day.","quality":0.9,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T14:06:23.597Z"},{"action":"verification","result":"7 files, tsc 0 errors, eslint 0 errors. Runtime: confidence bands widen correctly (±15%→±28.5%), threshold classification maps to all 5 levels, correlated river transforms correctly (linear ratio 0.3 + offset 2). Cascading prediction loop injects predicted flows into flowByDate for subsequent days.","quality":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T14:09:09.052Z"}],"startedAt":"2026-04-05T14:06:16.324Z","success":true,"endedAt":"2026-04-05T14:09:11.253Z","feedback":"Phase 5 was the fastest — 7 files, all components work first try. 4 parallel agents effective for independent modules (confidence, threshold, correlated, forecast)."}

Phase 5 complete. 7 files in lib/prediction/. Cascading prediction: Day+1 uses observed flow, Day+2+ uses predicted flow for lag features. Confidence bands: ±15% base widening with horizon. Threshold classification against 5 paddling levels. Correlated river estimation via discriminated union (linear/offset/power). Main entry: generateForecast(PredictionInput) → ForecastResult with DailyForecast[] including flow, confidence interval, threshold status.

Trained on 3 real Quebec rivers (060601 Ha! Ha!, 061029 Aux Sables, 061502 Métabetchouane). 2022-2025 data, 4383 feature rows, 3570 flow readings. Results: Test NSE=0.981, Test MAPE=9.7%. 53 trees (early stopped). Model saved to datas/models/flow-model.json. Data source: MELCCFP Quebec hydrometric service, parsed from datas/*.txt to datas/parsed/*.csv.

{"id":"traj-1775400516522-nxbmo8","task":"Phase 8: Web UI — Next.js 16 dashboard showing river forecasts with charts and confidence bands","agent":"architect","steps":[{"action":"architecture-design","result":"UI plan: dashboard (river list cards) + river detail (7-day forecast table with confidence bands). Server components fetch data directly. API routes for future client use. Shared data layer in lib/data/rivers.ts. Next.js 16: params are Promises, must await.","quality":0.9,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T14:49:22.114Z"},{"action":"verification","result":"tsc 0 errors, eslint 0 errors. Dev server tested: dashboard renders 3 rivers, API /api/rivers returns JSON, API /api/rivers/060601/forecast returns 7-day forecast with real weather data. River detail page renders forecast table with confidence bands.","quality":0.95,"timestamp":"2026-04-05T14:52:28.981Z"}],"startedAt":"2026-04-05T14:48:36.522Z","success":true,"endedAt":"2026-04-05T14:52:30.415Z","feedback":"Phase 8 UI built with 3 parallel agents. Dashboard + detail page + API routes all work first try with real data."}

Phase 8 complete. Web UI: dashboard at / (3 river cards with last flow), river detail at /rivers/[id] (7-day forecast table + visual bars + confidence bands). API routes: GET /api/rivers, GET /api/rivers/[id]/forecast. Data layer: lib/data/rivers.ts with React.cache. All server components, no client JS. Tailwind CSS 4 dark mode compatible.

{"id":"traj-1775413764579-sh0shq","task":"Build hourly predictions: diurnal profile from CEHQ 15-min data + daily model → hourly output","agent":"architect","steps":[],"startedAt":"2026-04-05T18:29:24.579Z","success":true,"endedAt":"2026-04-05T18:32:12.588Z","feedback":"Diurnal profile from CEHQ 15-min data successfully computed and applied. Real hourly variation visible — snowmelt rivers peak midday. Hourly chart shows observed (solid blue) transitioning to predicted (orange dashed) with confidence bands."}
